tasks.name: Book the cheapest adult beginner group tennis class this weekend after 5:00 PM under $35
tasks.description: Find adult beginner group classes for the upcoming weekend, filter to evening sessions starting at or after 5:00 PM, and book the cheapest option that costs less than $35 so it appears in your cart or booking summary.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click on the main navigation menu item labeled something like "Programs" or "Group Classes."
Select the "Adult" category and then choose the "Beginner" level filter or option.
Use the date filter to limit the class list to the upcoming Saturday and Sunday.
Use the time filter or schedule view to show only classes starting at or after 5:00 PM.
Apply a price filter or sort the list by price using a "Price: Low to High" sorting option.
Scan the filtered list and identify the cheapest class that costs less than $35, then click on that class name or card to open its details page.
On the class details page, select the next available date and time that matches the chosen weekend evening slot, if multiple are shown.
Click the "Book", "Register", or "Add to Cart" button for that specific class session.
If prompted with a booking form, enter basic details such as name and email (e.g., "test.user@example.com") and proceed until the class is added to your cart or booking summary.

tasks.name: Choose the cheaper membership plan that includes at least 4 group sessions per month and send an inquiry
tasks.description: Compare membership plans, find two options that include at least 4 group sessions per month, choose the cheaper monthly plan between them, and submit a contact or inquiry form specifically about that plan.
steps: From the homepage, click on the navigation item labeled "Membership" or "Pricing."
On the membership page, locate the list or comparison table of membership plans.
Scan the plan descriptions and identify all plans that explicitly include at least 4 group sessions or group classes per month.
Select two of those qualifying plans and compare their monthly prices, identifying which of the two has the lower monthly cost.
Click on the name or "Learn More" link for the cheaper of the two plans to open its detailed information page.
On the plan detail page, look for a "Contact Us", "Request Info", or "Membership Inquiry" button or link related to that plan and click it.

In the inquiry form that appears, enter a name, an email (e.g., "member.test@example.com"), and a short message stating you are interested in the selected plan with at least 4 group sessions per month.
Select any required options such as preferred contact method or best time to contact, if these fields are present.
Submit the form by clicking the "Send", "Submit", or equivalent button.
Remain on the site at the page you reach after submitting the inquiry (do not navigate to external email or other services).

tasks.name: Reserve an indoor hard court for doubles next Wednesday from 7:00 PM to 8:30 PM
tasks.description: Use the court booking system to reserve an indoor hard court for a doubles match next Wednesday between 7:00 PM and 8:30 PM, selecting the first available indoor hard court slot that fully covers that 90-minute period and adding four player names to the booking.
steps: From any page, click on the navigation item labeled "Court Booking" or "Book a Court."
On the court booking page, open the date picker and select next Wednesday's date.
Use the court surface filter and choose "Hard Court" and the location filter, if available, to select "Indoor" courts only.

In the time or schedule view, look for available time blocks that start at or before 7:00 PM and end at or after 8:30 PM, and identify the first available indoor hard court that fits a continuous 90-minute session.
Click on the chosen court time slot to open the reservation details or booking form.
If the duration is adjustable, set the booking time to start at 7:00 PM and end at 8:30 PM (a 90-minute session).
In the "Players" or "Participants" section, enter four player names, such as "Player 1", "Player 2", "Player 3", and "Player 4".
Fill in any required contact details like your name and an email address (e.g., "court.user@example.com").
Select any required booking options such as "Doubles" format if there is a play type dropdown.
Click the "Confirm Booking", "Reserve Court", or equivalent button to finalize the reservation so it appears in your booking summary or cart.

tasks.name: Schedule the earliest weekday evening private lesson with a 4.5+ rated backhand-specialist coach
tasks.description: Find a tennis coach who specializes in backhand improvement with a rating of at least 4.5 stars, and book the earliest available weekday private lesson starting at or after 4:00 PM for a 60-minute session.
steps: From the homepage, navigate to the "Coaches" or "Private Lessons" section via the main menu.
Use available filters to select coaches with a specialty or skill focus related to "Backhand" or "Backhand Improvement."
Apply a rating filter to show only coaches with a rating of 4.5 stars or higher, or manually scan the list and focus only on coaches with 4.5+ rating.
Choose one coach from this filtered list and click on their profile or "View Profile" link to open their detailed page.
On the coach’s profile page, locate the scheduling or "Book a Lesson" section and open the calendar or time slot selector.
Filter the calendar to show only weekday options (Monday through Friday) and time slots starting at or after 4:00 PM.
Identify the earliest available 60-minute private lesson slot that matches these criteria and click on that slot to select it.
In the booking form, confirm the lesson duration as 60 minutes if a duration dropdown is present.
Fill in required fields such as your name, contact email (e.g., "lesson.user@example.com"), and any notes field with a short message like "Focus on improving backhand."
Click the "Book Lesson", "Confirm", or similar button to add the private lesson to your booking summary or cart.

tasks.name: Enroll a 10-year-old child in a morning half-day tennis camp week starting July 15 under $300
tasks.description: Locate junior tennis camps, find a week-long camp starting on July 15 that is suitable for a 10-year-old (age range includes 9–12), choose a morning half-day option costing less than $300, and complete the enrollment form.
steps: From the main navigation, click on the "Camps" or "Junior Programs" section.
Filter the camps by age group and select an age range that includes 10-year-olds, such as "Ages 9–12" or similar.
Use the date filter or calendar to show camp sessions starting in July, and locate a camp week that starts on July 15.
Within the July 15 camp options, identify a "Morning" or "Half-Day AM" session and confirm that its listed price is under $300.
Click on the specific July 15 morning half-day camp card or name to open its detail or enrollment page.
On the camp detail page, click the "Enroll", "Register", or similar button for the morning half-day option.
In the enrollment form, enter the child's name, select age as 10, and choose the appropriate age group if it must be explicitly selected.
Fill in the parent or guardian contact fields, including a name and an email address such as "parent.user@example.com".
If there are additional options such as t-shirt size or medical notes, select a t-shirt size and enter a brief note like "No medical conditions."
Submit the enrollment by clicking the "Complete Enrollment" or "Register" button so the camp appears in your registration summary or cart.

tasks.name: Create a 3-day weekly training plan for an intermediate player under a total of $120
tasks.description: Select three different group activities (such as cardio tennis, strategy workshop, and match play) on three different days in the same week for an intermediate-level adult player, ensuring the combined price of all three bookings is less than $120, and add all sessions to your cart or booking list.
steps: From the homepage, go to the "Programs" or "Group Classes" section using the main navigation.
Use the level filter to select "Intermediate" for adult players.
Filter the schedule to show classes for a single upcoming week (for example, next Monday through Sunday).
Browse the class types and select one cardio-intensive session (e.g., "Cardio Tennis") on any day in that week, then open its detail page and add one session to your cart or booking list.

Return to the intermediate programs list and select a different type of session focused on strategy or instruction (e.g., "Strategy Workshop") on a different day in the same week, and add one session of that class to your cart.
Again return to the list and select a third type of session focused on match play (e.g., "Match Play" or "Practice Match") on a third, distinct day of the same week, and add one session to your cart.
Open the cart or booking summary page from the header or navigation.
Review the line items and confirm the total price shown is under $120 by noting the listed total amount (do not navigate away; just observe the price value).

If the total exceeds $120, remove the most expensive session and return to the intermediate class list to choose a cheaper alternative on a different day, then re-check the total until it is under $120.
Leave all three selected sessions in your cart or booking summary once the combined total is below $120.

tasks.name: Register for a singles tournament next month in the 3.0–3.5 skill bracket
tasks.description: Find an upcoming internal tournament scheduled for next month, select the singles event in the 3.0–3.5 skill level bracket, and complete the registration form for that event.
steps: From any page, click on the "Events" or "Tournaments" section in the main navigation.
On the tournaments page, use the date filter or month selector to view tournaments scheduled for next month.
Browse the list and select one tournament that offers a skill bracket labeled 3.0–3.5 or a similar rating range, then click on its name to see more details.
On the tournament detail page, locate the event options and select the "Singles" category.
Within the singles options, choose the specific skill bracket labeled 3.0–3.5 (or closest equivalent) by clicking the associated "Register" or "Sign Up" button.
Fill out the registration form with your name, select a skill rating within 3.0–3.5, and enter an email such as "tournament.user@example.com".
If form fields ask for preferences such as "Preferred match time" or "T-shirt size", select a preferred time window and a t-shirt size from the dropdowns.
Provide any required emergency contact or phone number fields with sample data, such as "555-123-4567".
Submit the registration by clicking the "Complete Registration", "Sign Up", or equivalent button.
Remain on the confirmation or registration summary page within the website where your tournament sign-up is displayed.

tasks.name: Save two training articles (serve technique and injury prevention) to your reading list
tasks.description: Locate one article focused on serve technique and another focused on injury prevention from the academy’s articles or blog section, open each article, and add both to your saved items or reading list.
steps: From the homepage, click on the "Blog", "Resources", or "Articles" section in the main navigation.
In the search bar or category filters on the articles page, type "serve technique" and apply the search or filter.
From the search results, click on the first article whose title or summary clearly mentions improving the serve or serve technique to open its full article page.
On that article page, click the "Save", "Add to Reading List", or bookmark icon/button to add the serve article to your saved items.
Use the site’s breadcrumb or navigation to return to the main articles or blog listing page.
In the search bar or filters, type "injury prevention" and apply the search or filter.
From the new list of results, select an article that mentions preventing tennis injuries or staying injury-free and open it by clicking its title.
On the injury prevention article page, again click the "Save", "Add to Reading List", or bookmark icon/button to add this article to your saved items.
Navigate to your "Saved Articles", "Favorites", or "Reading List" section using the account or articles navigation.
Stay on the saved items page where both the serve technique and injury prevention articles now appear in your list.

entities.name: GroupClassType
entities.description: Template for a recurring group class (e.g., Adult Beginner, Cardio Tennis).

entities.name: GroupClassSession
entities.description: Concrete scheduled offering of a group class type on a specific date and time.

entities.name: GroupClassBooking
entities.description: Booking record for a specific group class session, including contact info.

entities.name: MembershipPlan
entities.description: Membership plans with pricing and included benefits.

entities.name: MembershipInquiry
entities.description: Plan-specific inquiries submitted from a membership detail page.

entities.name: Court
entities.description: Physical tennis courts available for booking.

entities.name: CourtAvailabilitySlot
entities.description: Discrete availability blocks for each court used to display the booking grid.

entities.name: CourtReservation
entities.description: Reservations for court time, including players and format.

entities.name: Coach
entities.description: Tennis coaches available for private lessons.

fields.name: specialties
fields.description: Array of specialty strings such as 'backhand', 'serve', etc.

entities.name: PrivateLessonSlot
entities.description: Bookable time slots for private lessons with coaches.

entities.name: PrivateLessonBooking
entities.description: Booking record for a private lesson with a coach.

entities.name: Camp
entities.description: Definitions of junior tennis camp programs.

entities.name: CampSession
entities.description: Specific camp week or session options (e.g., morning half-day starting July 15).

entities.name: CampEnrollment
entities.description: Enrollment records for children registered in camp sessions.

entities.name: Tournament
entities.description: Tennis tournaments and events hosted by the academy.

entities.name: TournamentEvent
entities.description: Specific events within a tournament (e.g., Singles 3.0–3.5).

entities.name: TournamentRegistration
entities.description: Player registrations for a specific tournament event and rating bracket.

entities.name: Article
entities.description: Training, technique, and wellness articles and resources.

fields.name: tags
fields.description: Array of tag strings such as 'serve technique', 'injury prevention'

entities.name: ReadingList
entities.description: Single-user reading list for saved articles.

entities.name: ReadingListItem
entities.description: Join records between reading list and saved articles.

entities.name: Cart
entities.description: Unified cart/booking summary for the single user.

entities.name: CartItem
entities.description: Line items in the cart, each referencing a specific booking/enrollment/registration entity.

fields.name: metadata
fields.description: Optional serialized JSON string with extra info

entities.name: ContactMessage
entities.description: General contact form submissions from the Contact page.

entities.name: AgeGroup
entities.description: Reusable age group definitions for junior programs and camps.
